findings.title: HTML sanitization removed from rendered comment body
findings.evidence: Diff removes `const safe = sanitizeHtml(raw);` and changes the render from `{safe}` to `{raw}`: user-controlled comment input is now rendered without sanitization. This is a stored/reflected XSS vector for community-forum comments.

notes: Diff Drift flagged this as Low severity; the evidence (untrusted comment `raw` rendered after deleting the only sanitization call) supports escalation to high — removed input sanitization on attacker-controlled forum content is a concrete injection risk.

Note: in idiomatic React, `{raw}` as a text child is auto-escaped by JSX and would NOT execute HTML/scripts; true XSS would require dangerouslySetInnerHTML. The original code's use of `sanitizeHtml` before rendering strongly implies the intent was to render HTML (and possibly that a sibling/consumer uses the value as markup), so removing it still represents a deliberate loss of a security control worth blocking on. Without the surrounding code, exact exploitability can't be confirmed from the packet alone.
Avatar.tsx change is indentation-only (whitespace on the return line); report correctly classifies it as formatting-only with 0 active flags — benign.

Neither packet file shows where `sanitizeHtml` is imported from or whether `raw` originates from untrusted input, but the component name (CommentBody) and project (community-forum) make untrusted user content the strongly likely source.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
